description_long: This object consists of four metal canteens arranged side by side, each with a rounded, slightly flattened body and a small neck topped with a screw cap. The canteens display a weathered, tarnished surface with visible patches of discoloration and wear, suggesting extensive use or age. The metal appears to be dull and matte, with some areas showing more pronounced darkening or corrosion. Each canteen has a small metal loop attached to the cap, likely for securing the cap to the body with a chain or cord. The overall form is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. These canteens are typical of those used for carrying water or other liquids in outdoor or urban environments.
description: A set of four weathered metal canteens with screw caps and tarnished, matte surfaces, showing signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded faces and screw caps of all four canteens, highlighting their tarnished and worn surfaces.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profiles of the canteens, with caps and necks aligned in a row.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the backs of the canteens, similar in shape to the front but with less visible detail on the caps.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the canteens' profiles and the alignment of their necks and caps.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Visible tarnishing, discoloration, and surface wear; no major dents or cracks.
